input.background: Two studies report results with P-values of <0.001 and =0.02. The question explores why it is not necessarily true that the former study has found a stronger effect than the latter, as discussed in section 16.3.6.
input.question: Why is it not necessarily true that a study with P<0.001 has found a stronger effect than a study with P=0.02?

output.answer: The P value is a measure of the strength of evidence against the null hypothesis. It does not indicate the magnitude of the observed effect.
